mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP signals growing corporate earnings and economic momentum, lifting equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDP growth are not significantly above zero (t-test p > 0.10) over a 5-year walk-forward period.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4% indicates tight monetary policy that raises discount rates and borrowing costs, depressing equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, average 20-day forward return is positive or not significantly negative (one-tailed t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment combined with a low federal funds rate signals a robust but non-overheating economy, boosting confidence and equity prices.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: When PAYEMS growth > 0.2% mom and FEDFUNDS < 3%, the proportion of positive 20-day returns is ≤ 0.5 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: With unemployment already low (below 5%), any further rise signals labor market weakness and potential recession, leading to negative equity returns.
failure_condition: In the low-unemployment regime (UNRATE < 5%) with a rise, 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of the time or mean return > 0 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production signals contracting manufacturing activity and economic weakness, which negatively impacts short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Following monthly declines, 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of cases over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 1.3 million units indicate a strong residential real estate sector and consumer demand, supporting economic growth and equity prices.
failure_condition: When triggered, 20-day forward S&P 500 return is negative or zero in more than half of observations over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in industrial production and retail sales signal broad-based economic strength, creating a positive backdrop for equities.
failure_condition: The joint signal (both series positive mom) fails to produce an average 20-day return above 0.5% over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: An above-trend increase in the consumer price index signals rising inflation pressure, leading to expectations of tighter Fed policy and negative equity returns.
failure_condition: After CPI mom > 0.3%, the 20-day forward return distribution is not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.05).
